result.reasoning: The pattern of 3 gray markers consistently appearing at positions (0,1), (1,1), (2,1) in the output strongly suggests they serve as structural anchors. The non-zero values are then extracted from the input based on their relationship to these anchors.
next_steps: Analyze the exact mapping from anchor positions to extracted cells
Determine the extraction radius or pattern around each anchor
Verify the pattern holds across all training examples

hypothesis_gen.summary: Generated 3 hypotheses for task 137eaa0f. Best hypothesis: Diagonal Anchor Extraction (confidence 0.8)

findings.color_5_role: Color 5 is a CENTER MARKER that anchors the output grid. It always appears at output[1][1].
findings.output_structure: Output is always 3x3 grid. The center (1,1) is always color 5.
findings.region_extraction: Each colored region from input is mapped to a specific position in the 3x3 output based on its relative position to color 5.

spatial_mapping.description: Regions are mapped to output based on their position relative to the central color 5:
spatial_mapping.top_left: Region positioned above AND to left of any 5 → output[0][0]
spatial_mapping.top_right: Region positioned above AND to right of any 5 → output[0][2]
spatial_mapping.left: Region positioned to left of 5 (same row) → output[1][0]
spatial_mapping.right: Region positioned to right of 5 (same row) → output[1][2]
spatial_mapping.bottom_left: Region positioned below AND to left of any 5 → output[2][0]
spatial_mapping.bottom_right: Region positioned below AND to right of any 5 → output[2][2]

findings.critical_insight: There may be multiple color 5 markers in the input, but they all map to the same center position in output. The relative positioning determines the output location.

solution_approach.step_1: Find all color 5 positions in input
solution_approach.step_2: Identify all colored regions (connected components of non-zero, non-5 colors)
solution_approach.step_3: For each region, determine its relative position to the nearest color 5
solution_approach.step_4: Map each region to the corresponding position in the 3x3 output grid

data.name: Diagonal Anchor Extraction
data.description: The 3 gray markers (5s) serve as diagonal anchors. They define a 3x3 output grid where each anchor becomes a column in the output. The output column j contains values extracted from input near anchor j.
implementation_hints: Find all 3 gray markers in input
Sort them by position (e.g., row then column)
For each anchor, extract nearby values to form column j of output
The exact extraction pattern needs to be determined from examples

data.description: The gray markers appear at specific positions in the output (column 1, rows 0-2). The transformation extracts exactly 9 specific input cells based on the positions of the 3 markers and maps them to the 3x3 output.
implementation_hints: Use these positions to determine which 9 input cells to extract
Apply a fixed mapping pattern to place them in 3x3 output

data.name: Marker-Guided Component Selection
data.description: The gray markers identify which non-zero regions to extract. Each marker is associated with a specific region or set of cells, and the output combines these regions into a 3x3 grid.
implementation_hints: For each marker, identify associated non-zero cells
Combine these cells into 3x3 output grid
